subject: Geography
title: Climate Refugees and Future Borders
description: A quick win: understand Climate Refugees and Future Borders.

hook.text: By 2050, up to 200 million people may be displaced by rising seas and extreme heat.

buildup.text: Small island nations like Tuvalu and Kiribati face complete submersion this century.

discovery.text: Kiribati has already purchased land in Fiji as a potential new homeland.

twist.text: International law has no category for climate refugees—they have no legal right to cross borders.

climax.text: The borders of the future will be shaped by climate as much as by politics.

punchline.text: When your country drowns, which border will let you in?

quiz.question: Why has Kiribati purchased land in Fiji?
options: Their islands may be submerged by rising seas
Fiji offered it as a gift
